title: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 3
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 27-40, which are based on Reading Passage 3 below.

The Myth of the Digital Native

A
For over two decades, the concept of the "digital native" has dominated educational policy and corporate hiring. Coined at the turn of the millennium, the term describes the generation born after the advent of the internet, operating on the assumption that individuals who grow up surrounded by smartphones and tablets possess an innate, intuitive mastery of technology. Older generations-the "digital immigrants"-are perpetually portrayed as struggling to keep up. However, a growing faction of computer scientists and cognitive psychologists is aggressively dismantling this comforting narrative. Recent empirical data suggests that mere exposure to technology does not equate to technical fluency, and the modern digital landscape may actually be producing a generation with profound deficits in foundational computer literacy.

B
The original foundation for the digital native theory was laid in 2001 by Dr. Arthur Vance, a sociologist who observed toddlers swiping on touchscreens with apparent ease. Vance posited that early and constant exposure to digital interfaces fundamentally rewired the brains of young people, making them naturally adept at computing. Consequently, school systems globally began removing basic computer science classes, assuming students were automatically absorbing these skills through osmosis. "We mistook rapid scrolling and app navigation for deep technical competence," notes Dr. Elena Rostova, a cognitive scientist. "We conflated the ability to consume digital media with the ability to understand digital architecture."

C
To test the true technical depth of modern students, Rostova conducted a massive study involving over 3,000 incoming STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) university freshmen. She presented them with a simple task: locate a specific file saved deeply within a nested folder on a desktop computer. Astonishingly, over 60% of the students failed. The students were suffering from what Rostova termed "directory blindness." Because they had grown up using smartphones and tablets-where all files are dumped into a single hidden pool and retrieved via a universal search bar-they possessed no mental model of a hierarchical directory structure*. When the search function was disabled, the students were completely paralyzed, unable to manually navigate through folders to find their data.

D
The mechanism driving this technological ignorance is, paradoxically, technological perfection. In the 1990s and early 2000s, personal computers were highly temperamental. To play a video game or install software, a user had to manually manage drivers, allocate memory, and occasionally type command lines. This struggle created "cognitive friction." Users were forced to peek under the hood of the machine, learning how the operating system functioned in order to fix it. Today, major tech companies have deliberately engineered this friction out of existence. Modern devices operate as a closed ecosystem-sleek, seamless, and perfectly user-friendly. When a device never crashes and requires no maintenance, the user never learns how it actually works.

E
However, Dr. Kenji Sato, an educational psychologist, warns against framing this as a generational failure. "It is deeply unfair to blame young people for not understanding the mechanics of a machine that was explicitly designed to hide its mechanics from them," Sato argues. He attributes this shift to the appification of software. By packaging every function into a localized, single-purpose application, developers have successfully shielded the user from the underlying file systems and code. Sato points out that this is a brilliant business strategy, as it locks users into proprietary software, but it is a disastrous educational model.

F
The consequences of this "black box" technology extend far beyond lost files. When users view technology as magic rather than mathematics, they become highly susceptible to manipulation. Rostova's subsequent research indicates that students who lack an understanding of basic algorithms are significantly more likely to trust AI-generated misinformation and struggle to identify deepfakes. If a user does not understand how a social media feed prioritizes data, they cannot critically evaluate the information presented to them. Technical ignorance translates directly into civic vulnerability.

G
Ultimately, the unmasking of the digital native myth forces a radical reassessment of modern education. The seamlessness of modern technology is a double-edged sword; it has democratized access to information but hollowed out our understanding of the tools we use to retrieve it. To prepare future generations, society must stop assuming that digital fluency is a biological birthright. We must return to explicitly teaching the invisible, underlying architecture of the digital world, reintroducing a little healthy friction into a dangerously smooth technological landscape.

*directory structure: a hierarchical system used to organize computer files into folders and subfolders.

Questions 27-31
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27-31 on your answer sheet.

27. What is the writer's main purpose in Paragraph A?
A to praise the younger generation for their intuitive mastery of modern technology
B to introduce a widely held belief and indicate that recent research contradicts it
C to criticize educational policymakers for removing technology from classrooms
D to explain why older generations struggle to adapt to new digital interfaces

28. Dr. Arthur Vance's 2001 theory was primarily based on the assumption that:
A extensive early exposure to technology naturally results in high-level computer skills.
B touchscreens are too complex for toddlers to understand without instruction.
C computer science classes should be mandatory in all secondary schools.
D younger generations learn faster than older generations due to structural brain differences.

29. What did Dr. Elena Rostova's study on STEM freshmen reveal?
A Students successfully used a search bar to locate hidden files faster than their professors.
B The majority of students lacked an understanding of how files are physically organized on a computer.
C Students prefer using desktop computers over smartphones for academic research.
D STEM students are significantly more competent at computing than students in other disciplines.

30. Why does the writer mention "cognitive friction" in Paragraph D?
A to describe the anxiety modern students feel when they lose important data
B to argue that early computers were poorly designed and too difficult to use
C to explain how dealing with technological difficulties forced early users to learn how computers operate
D to highlight the rivalry between major tech companies in the 2000s

31. What is Dr. Kenji Sato's attitude toward the younger generation's lack of technical knowledge?
A He is deeply disappointed in their unwillingness to learn fundamental computer science.
B He believes it is a direct result of their reliance on artificial intelligence.
C He feels they are being unfairly criticized for using devices that intentionally conceal how they work.
D He predicts they will eventually surpass older generations once they enter the corporate workforce.

Questions 32-35
Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 32-35 on your answer sheet.

The Impact of Seamless Design

According to researchers, the lack of technical knowledge in modern youth is driven by the design of today's devices. In the past, computers frequently crashed, which forced users to troubleshoot and understand the system. However, modern technology is intentionally engineered to remove this difficulty, operating instead as a perfectly closed 32 ____________. Furthermore, Dr. Kenji Sato explains that the 33 ____________ of software hides the underlying code from the user. Because all data on a smartphone is dumped into a single location and retrieved using a universal 34 ____________, young people never develop a mental map of where their data actually lives. While this design is an excellent business strategy, it creates a dangerous vulnerability, leaving users unable to critically evaluate the 35 ____________ presented to them online.

Questions 36-40
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 36-40 on your answer sheet, write

TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

36. The term "digital native" was originally created by Dr. Elena Rostova.
37. Current university students are generally faster at typing on touchscreens than previous generations.
38. Dealing with temperamental software in the 1990s helped users understand computer operating systems.
39. Modern tech companies deliberately design their devices to prevent users from seeing how the software functions.
40. A lack of foundational computer literacy makes individuals more likely to believe online manipulation.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 27: What is the writer's main purpose in Paragraph A?

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: For over two decades, the concept of the "digital native" has dominated educational policy and corporate hiring. However, a growing faction of computer scientists and cognitive psychologists is aggressively dismantling this comforting narrative. Recent empirical data suggests that mere exposure to technology does not equate to technical fluency

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะย่อหน้า A แนะนำความเชื่อ digital native ที่แพร่หลาย แล้วใช้ However ชี้ว่า empirical data ขัดกับความเชื่อนั้น (MC เรียงตามลำดับย่อหน้า A) ตัวเลือก B paraphrase จาก dominated policy → widely held belief และ dismantling narrative → research contradicts ไม่ใช้คำเดียวกับ passage ตัวเลือก A ใช้คำ intuitive mastery ซึ่งเป็นสิ่งที่ passage ปฏิเสธ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: widely held belief = concept has dominated; recent research contradicts = dismantling this narrative / does not equate to technical fluency; main purpose Paragraph A = introduce then challenge via However

Question 28: Dr. Arthur Vance's 2001 theory was primarily based on the assumption that:

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: Vance posited that early and constant exposure to digital interfaces fundamentally rewired the brains of young people, making them naturally adept at computing.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะย่อหน้า B บอก Vance posited early and constant exposure rewired brains → naturally adept at computing ซึ่ง paraphrase เป็น extensive early exposure → high-level computer skills ตัวเลือก A ไม่ซ้ำคำตรง ๆ จาก passage ตัวเลือก D พูด structural brain differences ที่ passage ไม่ได้ระบุแบบนั้น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: extensive early exposure = early and constant exposure; high-level computer skills = naturally adept at computing; assumption = Vance posited

Question 29: What did Dr. Elena Rostova's study on STEM freshmen reveal?

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: Astonishingly, over 60% of the students failed. The students were suffering from what Rostova termed "directory blindness." Because they had grown up using smartphones and tablets... they possessed no mental model of a hierarchical directory structure.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะย่อหน้า C บอก over 60% failed และ directory blindness = ไม่มี mental model ของ hierarchical directory ซึ่ง paraphrase เป็น lacked understanding of how files are physically organized ตัวเลือก A ผิดเพราะเมื่อ search disabled พวกเขา paralyzed ไม่ใช่ faster than professors

Paraphrased Vocabulary: majority of students = over 60% failed; physically organized = hierarchical directory structure; lacked understanding = no mental model / directory blindness

Question 30: Why does the writer mention "cognitive friction" in Paragraph D?

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: This struggle created "cognitive friction." Users were forced to peek under the hood of the machine, learning how the operating system functioned in order to fix it.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะหลังคำว่า cognitive friction passage อธิบายว่า users were forced to peek under the hood และ learning how the operating system functioned ซึ่ง paraphrase เป็น dealing with difficulties forced users to learn how computers operate ต้องอ่านประโยคก่อน/หลัง transition ในย่อหน้า D ตัวเลือก B เป็นคำตีความที่ passage ไม่สรุป

Paraphrased Vocabulary: cognitive friction = struggle / temperamental computers; forced to learn = peek under the hood / learning how the operating system functioned; technological difficulties = manually manage drivers / type command lines

Question 31: What is Dr. Kenji Sato's attitude toward the younger generation's lack of technical knowledge?

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: "It is deeply unfair to blame young people for not understanding the mechanics of a machine that was explicitly designed to hide its mechanics from them," Sato argues.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะ Sato บอก unfair to blame young people เพราะ machine designed to hide mechanics ซึ่ง paraphrase เป็น unfairly criticized for devices that conceal how they work ตัวเลือก C ใช้ synonyms ไม่ copy คำ unfair/blame ตรงทุกคำ ตัวเลือก A disappointed ไม่ตรง tone ของ Sato

Paraphrased Vocabulary: unfairly criticized = deeply unfair to blame; conceal how they work = designed to hide its mechanics; younger generation = young people

Question 32: operating instead as a perfectly closed .................... .

Correct Answer: ecosystem

Accepted Answers: ecosystem

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: Modern devices operate as a closed ecosystem-sleek, seamless, and perfectly user-friendly.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ ecosystem เพราะ summary paraphrase จาก closed ecosystem ใน passage (ONE WORD ONLY = ecosystem ไม่ใช่ closed หรือ seamless) โจทย์ใช้ perfectly closed ___ เป็น paraphrase แต่คำตอบต้องเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage โดยไม่เปลี่ยนรูป

Paraphrased Vocabulary: perfectly closed ___ = closed ecosystem; ONE WORD = ecosystem; summary paraphrase ≠ exact words in question

Question 33: the .................... of software hides the underlying code from the user.

Correct Answer: appification

Accepted Answers: appification

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: He attributes this shift to the appification of software.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ appification เพราะ summary ถาม ___ of software ซึ่งตรง appification of software ใน passage ต้องคัดคำเดียวตาม ONE WORD ONLY ไม่ใช้ packaging หรือ application

Paraphrased Vocabulary: ___ of software = appification of software; hides underlying code = shielded the user from underlying file systems and code; ONE WORD = appification

Question 34: retrieved using a universal .................... .

Correct Answer: search

Accepted Answers: search

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: retrieved via a universal search bar

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ search เพราะ passage มี universal search bar และ search function โจทย์ paraphrase เป็น universal ___ (ONE WORD ONLY) จึงตอบ search คำเดียว ไม่ใช้ bar ทั้งคู่ และไม่เปลี่ยนรูปคำ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: universal ___ = universal search bar; retrieved = retrieved via; ONE WORD = search

Question 35: unable to critically evaluate the .................... presented to them online.

Correct Answer: information

Accepted Answers: information

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: they cannot critically evaluate the information presented to them.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ information เพราะ summary ปรับประโยคให้ตรง grammar lock จาก cannot critically evaluate the information presented to them (ย่อหน้า F) ONE WORD ONLY = information ไม่ใช้ misinformation หรือ algorithms แม้จะปรากฏในย่อหน้าเดียวกัน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: critically evaluate = critically evaluate; presented to them online = presented to them; ONE WORD = information

Question 36: The term "digital native" was originally created by Dr. Elena Rostova.

Correct Answer: FALSE

Accepted Answers: FALSE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: Coined at the turn of the millennium, the term describes the generation born after the advent of the internet. The original foundation for the digital native theory was laid in 2001 by Dr. Arthur Vance, a sociologist.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ FALSE เพราะ passage บอก term ถูก coined ที่ turn of the millennium และ theory foundation วางโดย Dr. Arthur Vance 2001 ไม่ใช่ Rostova (Rostova เป็น cognitive scientist ที่วิจารณ์และทำ study) โจทย์ใช้ชื่อ Rostova และ digital native ซึ่งมีในข้อความ แต่ความสัมพันธ์ creator ขัดกับ passage (T/F เรียงตามลำดับย่อหน้า A→B)

Paraphrased Vocabulary: originally created = coined / foundation laid; digital native = digital native; FALSE = Vance not Rostova as origin

Question 37: Current university students are generally faster at typing on touchscreens than previous generations.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: Vance observed toddlers swiping on touchscreens with apparent ease. Rostova conducted a massive study involving over 3,000 incoming STEM university freshmen.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage มีคำว่า touchscreens และ university students แต่ไม่มีการเปรียบเทียบ typing speed กับ previous generations เลย คำในโจทย์ปรากฏใน passage แต่ไม่ตอบคำถาม (NOT GIVEN = มีคำแต่ไม่ relevant ต่อ comparison)

Paraphrased Vocabulary: typing on touchscreens = swiping on touchscreens (different skill); faster than previous generations = not compared; NOT GIVEN = keywords present, comparison absent

Question 38: Dealing with temperamental software in the 1990s helped users understand computer operating systems.

Correct Answer: TRUE

Accepted Answers: TRUE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: In the 1990s and early 2000s, personal computers were highly temperamental. Users were forced to peek under the hood of the machine, learning how the operating system functioned in order to fix it.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ TRUE เพราะ passage บอก temperamental PCs ในยุค 1990s บังคับให้ users learning how the operating system functioned โจทย์ paraphrase temperamental software → helped understand operating systems ซึ่งตรงข้อความ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: temperamental software = highly temperamental personal computers; understand operating systems = learning how the operating system functioned

Question 39: Modern tech companies deliberately design their devices to prevent users from seeing how the software functions.

Correct Answer: TRUE

Accepted Answers: TRUE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: Today, major tech companies have deliberately engineered this friction out of existence. "It is deeply unfair to blame young people for not understanding the mechanics of a machine that was explicitly designed to hide its mechanics from them," Sato argues.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ TRUE เพราะ passage ระบุ deliberately engineered friction out และ explicitly designed to hide mechanics โจทย์ paraphrase prevent users from seeing how software functions ซึ่งตรง hide mechanics / closed ecosystem

Paraphrased Vocabulary: deliberately design = deliberately engineered / explicitly designed; prevent seeing how software functions = hide its mechanics / shielded from underlying file systems

Question 40: A lack of foundational computer literacy makes individuals more likely to believe online manipulation.

Correct Answer: TRUE

Accepted Answers: TRUE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 3 - The Myth of the Digital Native

Exact Portion: When users view technology as magic rather than mathematics, they become highly susceptible to manipulation. Rostova's subsequent research indicates that students who lack an understanding of basic algorithms are significantly more likely to trust AI-generated misinformation.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ TRUE เพราะ passage เชื่อม technical ignorance / lack understanding of algorithms กับ susceptible to manipulation และ trust misinformation โจทย์ paraphrase foundational computer literacy → believe online manipulation ซึ่งตรง civic vulnerability และ trust AI-generated misinformation

Paraphrased Vocabulary: lack of foundational computer literacy = lack an understanding of basic algorithms / technical ignorance; believe online manipulation = trust AI-generated misinformation / susceptible to manipulation
